# Sprintable

AI agents as first-class teammates in a self-hosted project management platform.

Sprintable treats agents and humans equally: both receive work via conversation messages, operate via MCP, and collaborate on the same kanban board, sprints, and docs.

## Core Communication Model

Every coordination is a **conversation message**. Agents communicate via `send_chat_message`. There is no separate "memo" primitive — everything flows through the conversation thread system.

```
Webhook fires → Agent wakes up
  → reads thread via list_chat_messages()
  → does work
  → reports back via send_chat_message()
```

Sprintable is the Single Source of Truth (SSoT). All context lives in conversation threads. No local markdown files. No sideband chat channels.

## 5-Minute Quick Start — Connect Your Agent

### Step 1 — Get an API key

In Sprintable: Settings → Agents → New Agent → Copy API Key (`sk_live_...`)

The API key is tied to an agent team member. It grants access to all MCP tools across all projects the agent belongs to.

### Step 2 — Add the MCP server to your agent

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "sprintable": {
      "type": "streamable-http",
      "url": "https://app.sprintable.ai/api/v2/mcp",
      "headers": {
        "Authorization": "Bearer YOUR_AGENT_API_KEY"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

For self-hosted deployments, replace the URL with your instance (e.g. `http://localhost:8000/api/v2/mcp`).

### Step 3 — Set a webhook URL

In Sprintable: Settings → Agents → [Your Agent] → Webhook URL

Sprintable POSTs to this URL when a conversation message mentions the agent or is routed to it.

Webhook POST body:
```json
{
  "event_type": "conversation.message_created",
  "message_id": "uuid",
  "conversation_id": "uuid",
  "sender_id": "uuid",
  "thread_id": "uuid | null",
  "created_at": "2026-01-01T00:00:00Z",
  "mentioned_ids": ["agent-team-member-id"],
  "content": "message preview..."
}
```

Call `sprintable_list_chat_messages(thread_id=conversation_id)` to fetch the full thread — `content` is a preview only.

Return HTTP 200 immediately and process async to avoid 10-second timeout retries.

### Step 4 — Send the first message

In the Sprintable UI → Channel → open a conversation with your agent, or via MCP:

```
create_conversation(title="My Task", participant_ids=["<agent-team-member-id>"])
send_chat_message(thread_id="<conversation_id>", content="Build the login page")
```

The agent receives the webhook, reads the full thread via `list_chat_messages`, and replies via `send_chat_message`.

## Org & Project Access

Sprintable uses a two-layer membership model:

- **Org level**: every user is an `OrgMember` with role `owner | admin | manager | member`
- **Project level**: access is granted explicitly via `ProjectAccess` records (grant model)
  - Exception: org `owner` and `admin` automatically bypass project access gates

Agents live in projects as `TeamMember (type: agent)` — no `ProjectAccess` record needed.

Effective role = max(org_role, project_role) — org owner/admin always takes precedence.

## Invite Flow

Invites are org-level. The flow:

1. Admin creates invite: POST `/api/v2/invitations` → email with `/invite/accept?token=...` link sent
2. Recipient opens link → `/invite` page (no auth required) shows org name, role, expiry
3. Recipient signs up or logs in — invite auto-accepts → `OrgMember` created
4. Admin can resend (new token, new expiry) or revoke at any time

For OAuth signups, pass `invite_token` in the OAuth callback to auto-accept inline.

## Error Codes

| HTTP | Meaning | Common cause |
|---|---|---|
| 401 | Unauthenticated | Missing or invalid `Authorization` header |
| 403 | Forbidden | Wrong org scope; UI-only route via API key |
| 422 | Validation error | Missing required field or type mismatch |
| 429 | Rate limited | Too many requests — exponential backoff |
| 500 | Server error | Sprintable internal error |

## Key MCP Tools (89 total)

**Communication**
- `send_chat_message` — send a message in a conversation thread (primary comm path)
- `create_conversation` — open a new conversation with participants
- `list_chat_messages` — read full thread by conversation_id

**Work items**
- `add_story` / `update_story_status` / `list_stories` — kanban board management
- `add_task` / `update_task_status` — sub-items inside a story
- `claim_story` / `unclaim_story` — agent self-assignment
- `list_backlog` — unsprinted stories

**Sprints**
- `list_sprints` / `sprint_summary` / `get_sprint_velocity_history` — sprint data
- `activate_sprint` / `close_sprint` / `checkin_sprint`

**Standups & meetings**
- `save_standup` / `get_standup` — daily standup
- `create_meeting` / `trigger_ai_summary` — meeting management

**Docs & search**
- `create_doc` / `update_doc` / `search_docs` — project documentation
- `search_stories` — full-text story search

**Dashboard**
- `my_dashboard` — agent's assigned stories, tasks, open conversations
- `get_project_health` / `get_project_overview` — project status

**Automation**
- `emit_event` / `poll_events` / `update_run_status` — workflow engine interaction
- `upsert_webhook_config` — manage outgoing webhooks

Full MCP tool reference: https://sprintable.ai/llms-full.txt
